failedAssertions.reason: Foundry build/test passed and Hardhat config/tests are removed, but the active source tree still contains contracts/LendingCore.sol and additional legacy-name references, so the canonical rename assertion is not fully satisfied as written.

The canonical liquidation tx emitted HookFailed, no LiquidationNotified event was observed, and the correlator produced zero matches, so the live correlationId flow through HookRegistry to XCM is not proven on-chain.

Deposit, borrow, and liquidation are evidenced on the canonical deployment, but no repay transaction is recorded for the M11 proof window and the liquidation is not a second-position proof, so the full required lending cycle is not proven exactly as asserted.

XcmInbox duplicate protection is proven only for a synthetic receipt correlationId; the recorded ReceiptReceived correlationId does not match the liquidation correlationId, so the required same-correlation Liquidated -> LiquidationNotified -> ReceiptReceived chain is not proven.

SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE_AUDIT.md and docs/dualvm/dualvm_submission_final.md still contain active pre-M11 names and claims, and README still has residual active LendingCore references, so the architecture docs do not yet fully reflect the unified bilateral system.

The committed bilateral proof does not prove one connected async bilateral trace: the liquidation path emitted HookFailed, the receipt used a different correlationId, and the correlator produced zero unified traces.

appliedUpdates.description: Added bilateral-async-unified run notes covering fallback-RPC governance checks, current async-proof gaps, and strict rename/doc-audit caveats.
